ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Attachment presents file identity, metadata, and actions; safety guidance and lifecycle context remain application composition.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora adds an independently selectable safety explanation that keeps unverified or unsafe file handling explicit without forcing upload or scanning behavior.

visualSignature.summary: A literal-white workbench row uses ink dividers, strong file hierarchy, violet context, a stable status rail, shared focus seams, restrained corners, and reduced-motion handling.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers can disclose file safety state and recovery guidance without coupling the component to a scanner, uploader, or storage provider.

api.enableWhen: Set showSafetyGuidance true and provide the consumer-owned safety state and guidance.

disabledBehavior.ui: No safety badge or guidance region is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: File identity and ordinary actions remain unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No safety-specific callback or policy evaluation occurs.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No safety description or related status text enters the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated semantic, keyboard, axe, narrow, RTL, forced-colors, and reduced-motion evidence is present; manual screen-reader and touch-device records remain outstanding.

interactionEvidence.summary: Action semantics, optional-region removal, touch sizing, narrow layout, and state rendering have focused automated coverage.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual screen-reader and representative touch-device evidence is not recorded yet.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn catalog has no first-party emoji picker, so consumers typically compose popover, input, and collection primitives or add a separate dependency.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora supplies dependency-free, consumer-data-owned emoji search over a localized ARIA grid while retaining spatial keyboard navigation and bounded recent selection.

visualSignature.summary: The picker uses a precise white workbench grid, ink cells, green selection, violet context controls, shared focus seams, compact hierarchy, and coarse-pointer sizing.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional localized search filters consumer-supplied emoji labels and keywords without storage, telemetry, or a bundled data set.

api.enableWhen: Set searchable true and optionally control the search value.

disabledBehavior.ui: No search label, input, or empty-search recovery is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The complete supplied emoji collection remains directly navigable.
disabledBehavior.events: No search change callback is emitted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No search control or search-result context enters the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: ARIA grid semantics, roving spatial keyboard behavior, RTL, axe, forced colors, reduced motion, and narrow layout are automated; manual screen-reader and touch-device records remain outstanding.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled selection, search filtering, categories, recents, tones, Home and End, spatial arrows, RTL mapping, and enhancement removal have focused evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn applications commonly compose a native image with Aspect Ratio; load-state policy, explicit fallback recovery, and informative-versus-decorative typing remain consumer work.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora provides an optional stable load-status rail plus a consumer-owned source-policy boundary and discriminated informative/decorative semantics.

visualSignature.summary: The image frame uses a literal-white surface, ink outline, restrained corners, violet loading context, green ready state, and a non-jumping status rail with forced-colors mappings.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An optional stable rail explains loading, ready, failed, or source-policy-rejected states without causing layout jumps.

api.enableWhen: Set showStatusRail true and optionally customize status copy or observe state changes.

disabledBehavior.ui: No load-state status rail is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Native image loading, fallback replacement, and source policy still operate.
disabledBehavior.events: No status-rail-specific event is emitted; onLoadStateChange remains consumer-selectable.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No load-state status region or announcement is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Informative and decorative image typing, fallback semantics, axe, narrow layout, RTL, forced colors, and reduced motion are automated; manual screen-reader records remain pending.

interactionEvidence.summary: Loading, ready, native error, rejected-source fallback, and optional status removal are represented in focused tests and stories.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn catalog has no first-party image cropper, so crop geometry, input modality parity, preview, and serialization are application-owned.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora exposes the same normalized crop geometry through pointer movement, RTL-aware keyboard commands, and optional exact numeric editing while preserving a successful hidden form value.

visualSignature.summary: A measured white inspection workbench uses ink framing, violet crop context, green active confirmation, a distinctive focus seam, touch-sized controls, and restrained geometry.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional labeled numeric inputs expose exact horizontal, vertical, width, height, and zoom adjustments alongside direct manipulation.

disabledBehavior.ui: No numeric crop-control fieldset is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Pointer and keyboard crop movement, zoom bounds, and serialization continue unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No numeric-input change events are produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No numeric labels, spinbuttons, or fieldset grouping enters the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Keyboard, RTL, labeled numeric controls, axe, forced colors, reduced motion, and narrow layout are automated; manual screen-reader and touch-device records remain pending.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled geometry, keyboard increments, real pointer capture, movement and cancellation, canonical numeric updates, reset, preview, and guide removal have focused browser evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual assistive-technology and representative touch-device evidence are not yet recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Typography supplies prose styles, while parsing, sanitization ownership, streaming context, and renderer trust boundaries remain consumer decisions.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora makes the rendering trust boundary visible and safe by default: without an explicit React renderer, source is shown literally and never passed to innerHTML.

visualSignature.summary: Literal-white prose and code surfaces use ink structure, strong reading hierarchy, violet provenance context, a stable streaming rail, and restrained corners.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An optional visible boundary identifies consumer-rendered output and communicates that parsing and sanitization remain adapter responsibilities.

api.enableWhen: Set showRendererBoundary true when supplying a renderer.

disabledBehavior.ui: No renderer-boundary label or provenance rail is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The selected literal or consumer React rendering path is unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No boundary-specific callback or processing occurs.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No renderer-boundary context is added to the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Safe literal output, consumer React rendering, streaming context, axe, forced colors, reduced motion, RTL, and narrow layout are automated; manual screen-reader records remain pending.

interactionEvidence.summary: Literal fallback, renderer boundary, streaming summary, and clean removal of optional output have focused component and browser evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn catalog has no first-party Markdown editor; consumers generally compose Textarea, Tabs, buttons, and their own preview and paste/upload policies.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora keeps a native successful textarea as the source of truth while offering an independently selectable, consumer-rendered split or tab preview and IME-safe editing shortcuts.

visualSignature.summary: The editor uses a white structured workbench, ink separators, strong label hierarchy, green active state, violet preview context, shared focus treatment, and reduced-motion-safe controls.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers can add a tabbed or split React preview without replacing the native form textarea or importing a parser into the component.

api.enableWhen: Set previewLayout to tabs or split and provide renderPreview.

disabledBehavior.ui: No preview tab, preview pane, or preview toolbar is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The native textarea remains editable, successful, resettable, and serializable.
disabledBehavior.events: The preview renderer is never called and no preview selection event occurs.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No tablist, tab, or preview region enters the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native label, description, validation, keyboard shortcut, tab semantics, form reset, axe, RTL, forced colors, reduced motion, and narrow layout are automated; manual assistive-technology records remain pending.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled editing, native submission and reset, IME-safe bold/italic shortcuts, preview, toolbar, word count, and abortable consumer paste/upload adapters have focused evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual assistive-technology evidence is not yet complete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn catalog has no first-party media player; applications rely on native media controls or assemble custom playback, captions, chapters, and transcripts.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora preserves native audio/video controls and consumer-owned sources while adding an optional transcript disclosure linked into the player's accessible description.

visualSignature.summary: The native player is framed by literal-white ink structure, strong context hierarchy, violet chapter context, green active signals, touch-sized actions, and restrained details regions.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An optional native details disclosure exposes consumer-supplied transcript content and associates it with the media element.

api.enableWhen: Set showTranscript true and supply transcript content.

disabledBehavior.ui: No transcript disclosure, summary, or content region is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Native playback, captions, and any independently enabled chapters continue unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No transcript disclosure toggle event can occur.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The media element is not described by transcript content and no disclosure enters the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native controls, text tracks, transcript association, chapter buttons, throttled live updates, axe, forced colors, reduced motion, RTL, and narrow layout are automated; manual caption and screen-reader records remain.

interactionEvidence.summary: Native playback ownership, source rejection, transcript disclosure, chapter seeking, and optional live time announcements are represented in focused source and browser evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual caption, screen-reader, and touch-device evidence remains incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn catalog has no maintained rich-text editing engine; production use generally requires a dedicated engine and application-owned serialization policy.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora provides a deliberately narrow, identified and versioned adapter shell that owns form serialization and validation without pretending a homemade contenteditable surface is a production editor.

visualSignature.summary: The Labs shell uses a literal-white ink frame, clear type hierarchy, violet adapter provenance, green ready status, a stable rail, shared focus integration, and restrained corners.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An optional visible provenance rail names the active adapter and version so engine ownership is inspectable instead of implicit.

api.enableWhen: Set showAdapterBoundary true while supplying an identified RichTextEditorAdapter.

disabledBehavior.ui: No adapter identity or version rail is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The consumer adapter surface and serialized form value remain unchanged.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No adapter provenance description enters the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Shell labeling, description, validation, successful serialization, reset, axe, RTL, forced colors, reduced motion, and narrow layout are covered; editor-engine semantics remain adapter-owned and require adapter-specific manual evidence.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled serialization, form reset, validation, adapter identity, and clean optional-output removal are covered; production engine keyboard, clipboard, history, and selection behavior is intentionally not claimed by the shell.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: A production maintained-engine adapter, compatibility matrix, and engine-specific manual accessibility evidence are not yet supplied.

maturityAssessment.rationale: The adapter shell honestly satisfies the planned Labs boundary, but no production engine or broad editing behavior is claimed until adapter and manual evidence exist.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn catalog has no first-party signature control; pointer drawing, keyboard alternatives, form serialization, undo, and legal context are application-owned.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora adds a keyboard-operable normalized vector signature path, undo and reset, with independently selectable typed and file alternatives rather than making canvas input mandatory.

visualSignature.summary: A literal-white signing workbench uses ink borders, green completed state, violet alternative context, a distinct canvas focus seam, touch-sized controls, status structure, and restrained corners.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional visible instructions complement the canvas keyboard model: arrows move a normalized cursor, Space toggles drawing, and undo and clear remain native buttons.

disabledBehavior.ui: No visible keyboard-instruction rail is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Canvas keyboard operability remains available as a baseline accessible input path.
disabledBehavior.events: No instruction-specific event occurs; signing events are unchanged.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No extra keyboard-instruction description is associated with the canvas.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Canvas naming, keyboard cursor and marking, visible alternatives, native file input, status announcements, form reset, axe, RTL, forced colors, reduced motion, and narrow layout are automated; manual screen-reader and touch-device records remain.

interactionEvidence.summary: Real pointer capture and cancellation, keyboard signing, normalized vector storage, undo, clear, typed/file alternatives, controlled/uncontrolled use, serialization, and reset have focused browser evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual screen-reader and representative touch-device evidence are not yet recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.
